description_long: This is a vintage mechanical typewriter, predominantly constructed from black metal with a robust, boxy frame and exposed mechanical components. The front features a full set of round, white-topped keys arranged in a classic typewriter layout, with a visible typebar mechanism above. The top section houses a cylindrical platen roller, flanked by metal levers and knobs for paper adjustment and carriage return, with two red accent buttons. Side panels include glass or plastic windows, revealing some of the internal mechanisms. The rear and sides display intricate linkages and gears, emphasizing the device's mechanical complexity. A colorful circular emblem is visible on the lower right front, and the brand name 'CFKA' is printed on the platen cover.
description: A vintage black metal typewriter with round white keys, exposed mechanical parts, and a 'CFKA' brand label on the platen. It features a robust frame, visible gears, and a colorful emblem on the front.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the carriage, roller, and mechanical linkages, with the back panel and side windows visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the side window, carriage return lever, and the depth of the key array.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the full keyboard, typebars, colorful emblem, and the 'CFKA' label on the platen.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the side window, mechanical gears, and the profile of the key layout.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
